1) Layer 1 (Physical): The Physical layer is responsible for transmitting and receiving raw data streams over a transmission medium. An example of this technology is Ethernet.

2) Layer 2 (Data Link): This layer's main task involves error-free communication between two nodes on the same network, it includes MAC addressing & ARP protocol.

3) Layer 3 (Network): The Network layer deals with logical addressing, routing between different networks, and congestion control etc. An example technology would be IP (Internet Protocol).

4) Layer 4 (Transport): This layer’s main responsibilities are end-to-end communication error-checking, flow control, and quality of service. An example technology is the Transmission Control Protocol (TCP).
   
5) Layer 5 (Session): The Session layer establishes, manages, and terminates connections between applications. A common protocol at this level is NetBIOS.

6) Layer 6 (Presentation): This layer is responsible for data encoding & encryption to ensure that data sent from source can be accurately understood by a destination computer. An example of this technology would be the Secure Sockets Layer (SSL).
   
7) Layer 7 (Application): The topmost layer, Application provides network services to end-user applications such as web browsers or database software. HTTP
